channel.title: Thermodynamics on RadixArk Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Thermodynamics on RadixArk Blog

item.title: The Second Law of Thermodynamics
item.description:
At the heart of thermal engineering at RadixArk sits the Second Law of Thermodynamics. While energy is always conserved (First Law), it is not always useful.

Entropy Definition

Entropy \( S \) in statistical mechanics, as defined by Ludwig Boltzmann, relates the macroscopic state of a system to the number of microstates \( \Omega \):

$$ S = k_B \ln \Omega $$

Where \( k_B \) is the Boltzmann constant. This simple equation is inscribed on his tombstone, defining our modern understanding of irreversible processes.
